title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite and Mr. Quinn converse over Turkish coffee served by Ali. Mr. Satterthwaite reveals his intention to visit Doveton Kingsburn, a quiet country villa and estate owned by his old friend, Tom Addison. He describes his long history with Tom, who served in the foreign service and married a Spanish woman named Pilar. 

Mr. Satterthwaite details the family's history: Tom and Pilar had two daughters, Lily and Maria. Maria died in childbirth, leaving behind a daughter named Inez, who lives in the village with her doctor father. Lily married a fighter pilot, Wing Commander Simon Gilliat; they moved to Kenya and had a son, Roland. However, Lily died in a car accident, leaving Roland as an infant. Simon subsequently remarried a widow, and they had a son named Timothy. The two boys, Roland and Timothy, were raised like brothers.

Due to circumstances in Kenya, Simon, his second wife, and the two grown sons eventually returned to England and moved to the Doveton Kingsburn manor to live harmoniously with Tom. Mr. Satterthwaite, who is Lily's godfather, expresses a desire to see Roland, whom he has not seen since the boy was twelve. He notes that Tom is aging and has a limp, but remains content. While Mr. Satterthwaite reflects on the nostalgia of visiting the estate, he admits that one thing worries him. Mr. Quinn maintains an intense interest in the family details, prompting a moment of suspicion from Mr. Satterthwaite.
